site.title: Luke Duran
site.description: Technical Product Manager building innovative products at the intersection of technology and business.

chat.intro: Hi, I'm Luke! I'd love to connect in person, but this is a good place to start — ask it anything you'd ask me.

hero.name: Luke Duran
hero.valueProp: I specialize in the unglamorous work of making complex, data-heavy systems feel simple and delightful.
hero.currently: helping score 92M+ single-family homes across safety, comfort, operations, resilience, and energy systems.

about.paragraph: I’m a Technical Product Manager focused on building reliable, data-heavy platforms that enable high-quality product experiences at scale. My work centers on backend systems, data foundations, and applied AI — translating complex domains into systems teams can trust and products users can rely on. I care deeply about clarity, operational rigor, and execution, and I tend to stay closest to the parts of the work where system design, product decisions, and real-world constraints meet.

roles.title: Technical Product Manager
roles.impact: As a Technical Product Manager at Pearl, I lead strategy and development for the company’s core products - the Pearl SCORE and Pearl SCORE Report. I define product direction in collaboration with leadership, shape the roadmap across data and platform capabilities, and ensure our primary go-to-market vehicle supports revenue growth and long-term scalability.
highlights: Lead strategy and development for the Pearl SCORE and SCORE Report, the company’s primary revenue
Own the API layer and core platform capabilities enabling scalable partner distribution and product
Contributed to the architectural evolution from a certification-focused platform to a national data product spanning 96M+ single-family homes.

roles.title: Operations Manager
roles.impact: As the company’s first Product Manager, I helped establish the product function, define roadmap priorities alongside executive leadership, and build both internal platform capabilities and consumer-facing digital products that expanded revenue beyond physical retail.
highlights: First Product Manager; helped establish the product function and define roadmap priorities with executive leadership.
Led product development for TEG Workspace (now Cosmo), establishing the internal platform that powered rapid retail expansion and scalable operations.
Built and launched consumer-facing digital products (Remote Adventures, Unlocked) and branded activations for PwC, Disney+, Nickelodeon, and Amazon Prime Studios.
Partnered with executive and marketing leadership to optimize the company’s north star metric (“fill rate”), including launching dynamic pricing experiments through a custom booking platform.

roles.title: Manager, Data & Analytics
roles.impact: Launched 3 new product lines that generated $2M+ in revenue
highlights: Led product development for new experience formats
Collaborated with design and operations teams to launch new products

roles.title: Product Manager
roles.impact: Managed $10M+ product portfolio and improved customer satisfaction by 25%
highlights: Managed a portfolio of 5 products with $10M+ annual revenue
Built and led a cross-functional team of 12 engineers and designers
Launched 3 major features that improved customer satisfaction by 25%

roles.title: Founder
roles.impact: Founded and led DigitalTenn, a product and software consultancy focused on building scalable web and mobile platforms and data-driven systems for growing businesses. Partnered directly with founders and operators to translate business objectives into clear product strategy and technical execution.
highlights: Founded and led a product consultancy building scalable web and mobile platforms for growing businesses.
Owned product strategy and end-to-end delivery for custom web and mobile applications, guiding initiatives from discovery through launch to drive revenue growth and operational scalability.
Advised early-stage teams on product positioning, prioritization, and scalable system design under resource constraints.

projects.title: Pearl App & Registry
projects.summary: A comprehensive analytics platform that leverages machine learning to provide actionable insights for product teams.

meta.impactSummary: Product teams make data-driven decisions 3x faster; user engagement increased 45%.

context: When I joined Pearl, the company operated primarily as a certification business. The core value proposition centered around validating home energy performance through structured certification workflows. Over time, I began championing a benefits-forward approach to communicating home performance — one designed to resonate with homeowners beyond the sustainability sphere. Rather than framing value strictly around energy efficiency, I pushed for positioning home performance in terms of tangible homeowner outcomes.

That thinking initially took shape as conceptual pillars (Safe, Smart, Healthy, Efficient), and through collaboration with leadership and cross-functional teams, evolved into a more durable structure — Safety, Comfort, Operations, Resilience, and Energy (SCORE). While the framework was refined over time, it catalyzed a shift in how we defined the Pearl SCORE: not just as a certification artifact, but as a standardized, homeowner-relevant representation of home performance.

That shift in framing directly informed the design of the Pearl SCORE and Registry — defining them not as certification artifacts, but as scalable, homeowner-facing products capable of supporting distribution and long-term revenue growth.
businessChallenge: Defining the Pearl SCORE required more than packaging certification data — it required designing a scoring system that meaningfully represented a home’s performance across our pillars: Safety, Comfort, Operations, Resilience, and Energy. The ambition was to create a standardized, homeowner-relevant representation of home performance that could scale nationally, not just serve as an output of individual certifications.

The complexity was twofold. First, the SCORE needed conceptual integrity. It had to reflect real homeowner outcomes in a way that was defensible, explainable, and grounded in measurable signals across each pillar. Second, it needed to operate at national scale. We set out to generate scores for 96M+ single-family homes across the United States — many of which would never undergo a full Pearl certification.

That meant designing a system capable of operating with incomplete and inconsistent inputs. In many cases, the only available data sources were permit records, assessor data, public datasets, homeowner-reported information, and certification artifacts where available. Critical performance characteristics were often missing or only partially observable.

The core product challenge became clear: how do you generate a credible, standardized performance score when inputs are constrained — and still maintain consistency, transparency, and scalability across tens of millions of properties?

Solving this required defining defensible assumptions where data gaps existed, establishing consistent logic for imputing missing information, and balancing precision with scalability. The SCORE could not be arbitrary — but it also could not be perfect. It had to be directionally accurate, explainable to homeowners and partners, and resilient under real-world data limitations.
strategyTradeoffs: Built a comprehensive analytics platform with real-time data processing, machine learning-powered recommendations, and customizable dashboards.
resultsImpact: Enabled product teams to make data-driven decisions 3x faster and increased user engagement by 45% through actionable insights.

projects.content:
## Overview

Project Alpha is a cutting-edge analytics platform designed to help product teams make data-driven decisions. The platform processes millions of data points daily and provides real-time insights through an intuitive dashboard.

## Key Features

- Real-time data processing and visualization
- Machine learning-powered recommendations
- Customizable dashboards and reports
- API for third-party integrations

## Technical Highlights

Built with modern web technologies and scalable architecture, Project Alpha handles high-throughput data processing while maintaining sub-second response times for user queries.

projects.title: TEG Workspace
projects.summary: A modern collaboration platform that helps distributed teams work together more effectively through async communication and task management.
projects.content:
## Overview

Project Beta addresses the challenges of remote team collaboration by providing a unified platform for communication, task management, and project tracking.

## Key Features

- Real-time messaging and notifications
- Kanban-style task boards
- Video conferencing integration
- Automated workflow triggers

## Impact

Used by 50+ teams to improve collaboration efficiency and reduce meeting overhead by 40%.

projects.title: Project Gamma
projects.summary: A suite of tools that helps e-commerce businesses optimize their conversion rates through A/B testing and personalization.
projects.content:
## Overview

Project Gamma provides e-commerce businesses with powerful tools to test, optimize, and personalize their customer experience.

## Key Features

- Visual A/B testing builder
- Real-time personalization engine
- Conversion funnel analytics
- Customer segmentation tools

## Results

Helped clients increase conversion rates by an average of 22% through data-driven optimization.

hero.name: Luke Duran
hero.valueProp: Building, operating, and leaving things better than I found them — at work and at home.
hero.currently: helping my wife build a national romance-only bookstore brand out of the back of a trailer and teaching my toddler about the world.

about.paragraph: I’m a dad, a builder, and someone who likes to stay close to the work. I spend a lot of my time building and running small businesses, shipping ideas, and learning by doing — whether that’s working through messy systems, figuring things out as I go, or writing the occasional song when I need a creative reset. I’m most at home when I’m building something useful, taking responsibility for how it runs, and leaving things a little better than I found them — at work, at home, and everywhere in between.
